hero.title: 8 Days in Nepal.
hero.titleAccent: To the Sacred Garden Where the Buddha Was Born.
hero.intro: In the foothills of the Himalayas, in the place where Prince Siddhartha was born in 623 BCE, the garden of Lumbini holds one of the most spiritually powerful sites in the world. This eight-day Nepal journey weaves the birthplace of the Buddha through the great temples of Kathmandu, the Himalayan sunrise of Nagarkot and the serene lakes of Pokhara.

breadcrumb.label: 8 Days Nepal Lumbini Birthplace of Buddha

Entrance Fees to temples and sites

overview.heading: 8 Days Nepal + Lumbini: The Full Picture
body: Lumbini is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most important pilgrimage destinations in the world for the 535 million people who follow the Buddhist tradition. The exact spot where Prince Siddhartha Gautama was born — and where he later became the Buddha — is marked by the ancient Maya Devi Temple, the Ashoka Pillar and a sacred Bodhi tree. Around them, monasteries from every Buddhist nation on earth have built temples in their own tradition.

This eight-day itinerary is designed for travellers who want the complete spiritual and cultural spectrum of Nepal: the Boudhanath and Swayambhunath stupas of Kathmandu, the Himalayan drama of Nagarkot, the lakeside serenity of Pokhara, and the profound quiet of the Lumbini sacred garden.

highlights.heading: Nepal's Most Complete Spiritual and Cultural Journey

items.title: Lumbini — Birthplace of the Buddha
items.body: The Maya Devi Temple marks the exact birthplace of Prince Siddhartha, with the ancient footprint stone and the nativity scene bas-relief inside. The Ashoka Pillar — erected by Emperor Ashoka in 249 BCE — still stands in the garden. Walking the sacred zone at dawn, when the monks are chanting and the mist hangs over the Bodhi tree, is an experience that touches travellers of all faiths.
items.imageAlt: Lumbini sacred garden Nepal birthplace of Buddha Maya Devi Temple

items.title: World Monastery Zone
items.body: Around the central garden, 17 countries have built their own Buddhist monasteries in their own tradition — Thai, Chinese, Sri Lankan, Japanese, German, French, Tibetan. Walking through the monastery zone is a remarkable lesson in how one life story has expressed itself across dozens of cultures and centuries.
items.imageAlt: Lumbini monastery zone Nepal international Buddhist temples

items.title: Kathmandu, Nagarkot & Pokhara
items.body: The full classic Nepal circuit is included — Boudhanath's watching eyes, Pashupatinath's sacred ghats, a Nagarkot Himalayan sunrise and Pokhara's Phewa Lake beneath the Annapurna range. Lumbini adds the essential fourth pillar of the journey.
items.imageAlt: Boudhanath Stupa Kathmandu Nepal Buddhist pilgrimage

days.title: Arrive Kathmandu
days.description: Arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport and transfer to your Kathmandu hotel. Evening at leisure.

days.title: Kathmandu UNESCO Temples
days.description: Full-day private tour of Kathmandu's great UNESCO sites — Pashupatinath, Boudhanath, Swayambhunath and Durbar Square.

days.title: Bhaktapur & Nagarkot Overnight
days.description: Morning in medieval Bhaktapur, then drive to Nagarkot for sunset and overnight.

days.title: Nagarkot Sunrise & Fly to Pokhara
days.description: Pre-dawn Himalayan sunrise at Nagarkot, then fly to Pokhara.

days.title: Pokhara: Sarangkot & Phewa Lake
days.description: Sarangkot sunrise over the Annapurna range, World Peace Stupa boat ride and Phewa Lake.

days.title: Drive to Lumbini
days.description: Drive from Pokhara to Lumbini (approximately 4 hours). Afternoon orientation walk in the sacred garden.

days.title: Lumbini Sacred Garden — Full Day
days.description: Full day at Lumbini. Maya Devi Temple at dawn, Ashoka Pillar, Bodhi tree, the World Monastery Zone and the museum.

days.title: Lumbini to Kathmandu & Depart
days.description: Return to Kathmandu by road or short flight, then transfer to the airport for your international departure.

whyBook.heading: The Complete Buddhist Nepal, Arranged with Care
whyBook.body: Lumbini is not just a box to tick — it is one of the most genuinely moving places on earth, and visiting it well takes the right timing and the right context. Dhesu has been arranging pilgrim journeys through Nepal since 1988, and we know what the sacred garden looks like before dawn when the monks start chanting.

points.title: Private Tour, Your Schedule
points.body: Sacred sites need quiet. Your private guide and vehicle give you the garden without the group bus crowd.

points.title: A Malaysian Team to Call
points.body: We speak Bahasa Malaysia. Reachable throughout your eight-day journey.

points.title: Group & Family Pricing
points.body: Buddhist group pilgrimages are a speciality. Ask us for group rates.

sections.heading: Lumbini Nepal Tour Package from Malaysia — Birthplace of Buddha
sections.body:
Lumbini in southwestern Nepal is one of the most sacred sites in the world — the birthplace of Siddhartha Gautama, who became the Buddha. Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1997, the sacred garden contains the Maya Devi Temple (marking the exact birthplace), the ancient Ashoka Pillar erected in 249 BCE, and the sacred Bodhi tree planted on the spot where the Queen Maya Devi gave birth.

For Buddhist travellers from Malaysia, this is one of the holiest pilgrimages possible. For travellers of all faiths and none, the quiet of the garden and the weight of 2,600 years of history make it one of Nepal's most extraordinary experiences.

sections.heading: The World Monastery Zone
sections.body:
Around the central sacred garden, 17 countries have built their own national Buddhist temples and monasteries — Chinese, Thai, Sri Lankan, Japanese, German, French, Tibetan, Burmese and more. Each is in its own architectural tradition. Walking through the monastery zone is a remarkable experience — the same story, the same life, expressed in a dozen different architectural languages.

Dhesu recommends visiting the sacred garden at dawn, before the tour groups arrive, and spending the afternoon at the monastery zone. We structure the Lumbini day accordingly.

items.question: Why is Lumbini significant?
items.answer: Lumbini is the birthplace of Siddhartha Gautama (the Buddha), born in 623 BCE. It is one of the four most sacred Buddhist pilgrimage sites in the world — along with Bodh Gaya (enlightenment), Sarnath (first teaching) and Kushinagar (passing). Emperor Ashoka visited and erected his famous pillar here in 249 BCE. The site is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and remains an active pilgrimage destination for Buddhists worldwide.

items.question: What is the Maya Devi Temple?
items.answer: The Maya Devi Temple is built over the exact spot identified by archaeological excavations as the birthplace of the Buddha. Inside is a marker stone with the footprint of the infant Siddhartha and an ancient bas-relief depicting the nativity scene — Queen Maya Devi holding a branch of the Sal tree as the infant is born. The temple is open to visitors of all faiths.

items.question: How do we get from Pokhara to Lumbini?
items.answer: The drive from Pokhara to Lumbini takes approximately 4 to 5 hours by private car through the Terai lowlands. There is also a short flight option. Your private vehicle handles all inter-city transfers throughout the package.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Nepal?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders obtain a Visa on Arrival at Kathmandu's Tribhuvan International Airport. A 15-day tourist visa costs USD 30. Bring passport photos and the fee in cash. Dhesu provides full guidance with your booking.

items.question: Is this suitable for Buddhist group pilgrimages from Malaysia?
items.answer: Yes. Lumbini is a popular pilgrimage destination for Malaysian Buddhist groups and temples. Dhesu specialises in Buddhist group pilgrimages to Nepal, with experience managing groups from Malaysian Buddhist associations. Ask us for group rates and custom pilgrimage itineraries.
